title: Thermodynamics of homogeneous Universes: de Sitter, Bonnor-Melvin and static Einstein
abstract: In the theories, in which dynamic gravitational field emerges from the underlying matter fields, the gravitational field can be considered as a part of matter. Using this approach, we construct the thermodynamics of the homogeneous Universes -- the de Sitter Universe, the Bonnor-Melvin-$Λ$ Universe and the static Einstein Universe. It is demonstrated that although these three Universes have different types of matter fields (ordinary matter, magnetic field, gravitational field and vacuum energy), they have the same thermodynamic properties. Their energy densities obey the same equation, which contains the corresponding matter densities and the pairs of the thermodynamically conjugate variables. In Minkowski vacuum, where the ordinary matter and magnetic and gravitational fields are absent, this thermodynamic approach automatically leads to zero cosmological constant.
summary: This paper proposes a unified thermodynamic description for different types of homogeneous universes by treating the gravitational field as a matter field. It shows that the cosmological constant problem can be resolved via thermodynamic compensation in the Minkowski vacuum. Additionally, it identifies the de Sitter graviton as the second sound of a two-fluid system.
main_problem: Investigating the thermodynamics of homogeneous universes (de Sitter, Bonnor-Melvin, and Einstein static) and addressing the cosmological constant problem through an emergent gravity framework.
main_result: The author demonstrates that these three distinct universes share identical thermodynamic properties and that the cosmological constant naturally vanishes in the Minkowski vacuum due to thermodynamic compensation.
method: The paper employs a two-fluid hydrodynamic analogy (similar to Landau's superfluid model) and a generalized energy-momentum tensor construction within a pre-geometric/emergent gravity framework.
model_or_system: The study considers three homogeneous cosmological models: the de Sitter Universe (vacuum energy + thermal gravity), the Bonnor-Melvin-Lambda Universe (magnetic field + vacuum energy), and the static Einstein Universe (ordinary matter + vacuum energy).
key_observables: Generalized energy density and pressure, temperature (Hubble-related), entropy density, and the speed of second sound.
important_parameters: Ricci scalar curvature (R), gravitational coupling (K), 4-form curvature (q), and the equation of state parameter (w).
main_assumptions: The universes are treated as equilibrium thermodynamic states, and boundary effects in non-compact de Sitter space are ignored.
paper_structure: The paper begins by establishing the emergent gravity framework, proceeds to construct the thermodynamic formalism for the three specific universes, applies a two-fluid hydrodynamic analogy to identify gravitons as second sound, and concludes with the derivation of the zero cosmological constant in the Minkowski limit.
why_interesting: The use of Landau's two-fluid hydrodynamics and the identification of gravitons as 'second sound' provides a deep connection between relativistic cosmology and the many-body physics of superfluids.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
